transcript:
[00:00:12] Daniel: ok ok we're recording? yeah. ok cool. uhm so let's just — let's just go around real quick i don't wanna keep this longer than it needs to be cause i've got the board thing at eleven so

[00:00:23] Priya: do you want us to do the standard like what i did yesterday what im doing today or

[00:00:27] Daniel: nah just like — just blockers and the atlas stuff really, the rest we can do async. hugo you wanna start

[00:00:34] Hugo: yeah so um the migration script for the user table it's basically done, it's just — there's this weird thing with the timezone fields where like half of them are utc and half of them are local and i don't, yeah i don't know which is the source of truth honestly

[00:00:51] Daniel: ugh ok

[00:00:52] Hugo: so i kinda need someone to like dig through the old code or talk to whoever wrote it originally

[00:00:58] Daniel: that was — was that pablo? no pablo left. uhm

[00:01:03] Priya: i think it was actually a contractor

[00:01:05] Daniel: right right. ok hugo can you just — can you just pick one, document the assumption and we move on, like if it breaks something we'll catch it in staging

[00:01:14] Hugo: yeah ok i'll go with utc and write it up

[00:01:17] Daniel: cool. and like get that in before friday ideally because kenji's blocked on it right?

[00:01:23] Kenji: yeah im — i mean i can work on other tickets in the meantime but the api layer needs the new schema so

[00:01:30] Daniel: ok. friday hugo. cool. priya

[00:01:34] Priya: so the design system stuff is — rafa sent over the new tokens last night, im going through them now. one thing though is the new color palette has like accessibility issues on the dark mode, the contrast ratios are below aa on like three of the secondary colors

[00:01:51] Daniel: oh great

[00:01:52] Priya: yeah so im gonna — i can either flag it to rafa and wait or just fix it myself and send him a pr

[00:01:59] Daniel: just fix it, faster. but tell him after so he updates the figma file. actually you know what, can you also just ping sofia about it because she had opinions about the palette last time and i don't wanna get caught in that again

[00:02:13] Priya: ha ok will do

[00:02:15] Daniel: kenji

[00:02:16] Kenji: uhm so ive been on the rate limiting middleware, its like 80% done, i pushed a draft pr yesterday. um nora left some comments about the redis connection pooling that i wanna address before merging

[00:02:31] Nora: yeah sorry i was — i was a bit harsh in those comments actually it's fine, the approach is fine, i just want you to handle the reconnect case because we had that incident in march remember

[00:02:42] Kenji: right yeah ok

[00:02:43] Daniel: ok can you both sync on that this afternoon maybe? like just 15 min

[00:02:48] Nora: yeah

[00:02:48] Kenji: yeah sure

[00:02:50] Daniel: ok and kenji once that's merged can you take the timezone thing off hugo's plate next week, the followup work i mean, not the migration itself

[00:03:00] Kenji: yeah ok

[00:03:01] Daniel: nora what about you

[00:03:03] Nora: so the staging environment is back up, i had to — there was a whole thing with the iam roles, i won't bore you with it, but. uhm the bigger thing is the p99 latency stuff, the drift project, we agreed last week i'd put together a proposal and i havent really had time

[00:03:21] Daniel: yeah no thats fair you were firefighting

[00:03:23] Nora: yeah. so um can i push that to like, next wednesday? i wanna get it right not just throw something together

[00:03:30] Daniel: yeah fine. wednesday. put it on the calendar though so i actually look at it

[00:03:35] Nora: ok

[00:03:36] Daniel: also — sorry one more thing — the cost report for aws, finance is asking again, diego pinged me yesterday. can you just pull the numbers and send them to him

[00:03:46] Nora: this week?

[00:03:47] Daniel: yeah ideally by — i mean diego said end of month but the sooner the better, he's doing the q3 forecast

[00:03:54] Nora: ok i'll do it tomorrow probably

[00:03:56] Daniel: 🙏 thanks. tomas you've been suspiciously quiet

[00:04:01] Tomas: [laughs] yeah i mean i've just been heads down on the ranking model, the offline evals look really good actually, like the ndcg is up like 12% vs the baseline

[00:04:13] Priya: oh nice

[00:04:14] Tomas: yeah so im — the next step is to set up the shadow deployment, i need someone from backend to help me wire up the dual-write thing

[00:04:23] Daniel: hugo can you — actually no hugo's on the migration. uhm

[00:04:28] Hugo: i mean i can pair with him for an hour or two

[00:04:31] Daniel: ok yeah do that, like later this week, doesn't have to be today. tomas can you book something with hugo

[00:04:37] Tomas: yeah sure

[00:04:38] Daniel: and tomas — elena was asking about the model card thing, like the documentation for the ranking model, for the product review. did you ever do that?

[00:04:47] Tomas: [inaudible] kind of? i have a notion doc but it's not — it's not really polished

[00:04:53] Daniel: ok can you finish it before the product review on the 24th, elena needs it to walk sofia through

[00:05:00] Tomas: yeah ok 24th

[00:05:02] Daniel: cool. um. anything else? blockers? no? ok — oh wait, one thing, the on-call rotation for next month, i need someone to own setting it up, we've been doing it ad hoc and it's a mess

[00:05:18] Nora: i can do it

[00:05:19] Daniel: oh perfect, thanks. just like, get a draft and circulate it, no rush, just before the end of the month

[00:05:25] Nora: ok

[00:05:27] Daniel: ok we're good. oh by the way kenji are you coming to the offsite

[00:05:31] Kenji: yeah i think so my flight is on the —

[00:05:33] Daniel: cool cool ok bye everyone

[00:05:36] Priya: bye
